channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 159 replies — 636.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 159 • Updated: 2026-06-25 15:59 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62398876
>>62398868
https://voca.ro/1aI6fGYlNRNN

62398905
>>62394942
Don’t get that either. Even if you only bought full shares how could you not afford 200 dollars worth of shares? Is this under 10k net worth or under 100$ net worth

62399272
>>62398789
>Wavelength (λ) (THE SYMBOL IN HALF LIFE)

Distance between repeating points of a wave (e.g., crest to crest). Determines frequency and, for light, color.

>Amplitude:

Maximum height of the wave. Determines intensity (brightness for light, loudness for sound).

>Phase

Position of a wave within its cycle. Determines how waves add or cancel when combined.

>Polarization

Direction of oscillation of a transverse wave. For light, it is the orientation of the electric field.

62399358
Tomorrow we shall give predictive programming a try as photonics will likely be a difficult venture

62399394
10k- networth reporting in! You guys are too smart for me with all those grand ideas.

62400367
>>62398789
tl;dr

62401110
7 figure club. I already lost more than 10k today at market open. Stay poor fags

62401765
I just wrote a super long post with all sorts of trade basics and an emphasis for this group to define normal position exit conditions so you can tangibly ask anons "did you exit before, during, or after the standardized 4chan-exit signals?" Along with recommending covered calls for slow moving but trend holding markets like gold/BTc/xmr or oil when watching forex markets. 10y+dxy are good to watch too.
Blah blah blah
But this whole thread degenerated into trying to sell "art", and not every anon working together for every group success to contribute to all that participate, but rather instead for every anon to work on their own project, by themselves, but as a group selling the same products/watering down their labor's value.

>>62401110
This is who you should ask for advice. This anon only lost 1% of his shit n that's if he's only got 1m. Not a big deal. Don't you anons want to be able to lose 10k casually n be unbothered yet responsible?

62401809
>>62401765
I did it the hard way. Degree in finance. Work in finance. Almost 20 years. Been investing since the 08 crash. There's no quick trick or easy money. Crypto is bullshit. Just keep buying.

62401893
Six figures is truly hell, I miss being proud of 70k.

62403333
>>62401765
It was one art guy

62403337
>>62401765
This general also is a work in progress, I am hoping 10 generals from now we have a much better work flow, plan and ultimately success. In some capacity.

62404260
Bump

62404574
No point in this general. Fastest way to make 10k is just to work for a bit, you shouldn’t be investing

62404709
Bump

62404737
>>62373752
its a spacex thread?
its time to buy o better buy in few days one week

62405715
bump

62407354
Page 8 bump, /biz/ must be losing money again because there's an uptick in activity.

62407450
almost 9 months since i started my first job after college
this feels stagnant and joyless
what exactly do i need to do to escape wageslavery and poverty

62407931
Someone send me ten grand so I can join the club, thanks

62408558
>>62407450
What were you doing in 2017, 2021 and December of 2024?

62408565
>>62407354
Thank you, my wage job prevents me from dedicating my energies to this thread but I should have some time soon

62408584
>54 PBITD
So you're the dumb nigger who ruined this board eh?
Gonna claim everyones the same person and mass report threads/posts again lol
JFC this boards dead get off 4chan while you still can anyone left are pajeet scammers.

62408588
>>62408565
>>62407354
See he's a reddit pajeet scammer who samefags on a dead board and claims everyone is a liar lol.

62408611
>>62408584
Wtf are you on about dude, yes I made 54 posts over the span of 8 days. I'm surprised you're not taking your own advice and leaving 4chan.

62410044
Bump

62410051
Under 10k you just need to have a job and save cash.
You need cash flow and if you dont have shit a job is the only way to start.

62410731
Bump

62411520
>>62408558
>2017
still in high school
>2021
still in uni
>December of 2024
just graduated and was looking for jobs (i still had 0 dollars to my name at that point)

62411614
>>62373752
>>62373772
>>62373798
>>62373799
>>62373803
>post Maccari's "Cicero Denounces Catiline" (1888)
>crop out Catiline
Fuck you OP, I'm glad you're poor.

item.title: >380k nw >NEET >rentoid >not enough to buy a house without having to work again >not sure what to do >but, can't do noth… — 26 replies — 104.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>380k nw >NEET >rentoid >not enough to buy a house without having to work again >not sure what to do >but, can't do noth…

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-06-25 16:00 UTC

OP

>380k nw
>NEET
>rentoid
>not enough to buy a house without having to work again
>not sure what to do
>but, can't do nothing obviously

I have some business ventures starting up that make a little money, and should make more in a few years.

just wondering is anyone else in a similar situation?

Early replies (first 3)

62407791
how much is your rent? how much for utilities?

62407886
>>62407791
total monthly expenses are around $2600 or a little more depending on what I need, the money from investments and my business work helps cover that

62408170
>>62407772
If you didn't have enough sense to get a high paying career and/or your parents aren't rich, you shouldn't expect to ever have your own house.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62408181
>>62407772
>>62407886
Why do you make these gay fake threads, OP is from argentina and makes attention seeking threads and begging threads.
Blows my mind since nobody is here.

62408185
Not worth buying a house until you make it.

Rent somewhere cheap and go all in on risk assets.

62408210
>>62408170
>high paying career
I left it early for my health

>>62408181
I'm from the US. Looking for others like me to trade ideas, you don't need to reply.

>>62408185
People ruin their lives this way, I run a well-structured port that cannot be vanquished like that.

62408215
>>62408210
Same fucking faggot attention whore breakroom as always.
Dumb terminally online faggot.

62408222
>>62408215
This faggot OP is the biggest nigger on 4chan absolutely ruined this place.

62408233
>>62408215
>>62408222
can you show an example of this other guy? I don't spend much time here, so it's ironic that you call me terminally online when you are clearly well acquainted enough to be borderline stalking some specific person posting on a dead board.

62408514
>>62408210
Vax status

62408547
>>62408233
We all know it's you, fucking cocksucker lol
you ruined this fucking board.

62408593
>>62407772
if you have 380k you can afford 20% on a 300k house.
try buying a house with 40k in the broke rage, i would get laughed at. didn't know what i wanted until it was too late.

62408633
If you have less than 800k NW as a NEET you are relegated to a $600 studio apartment and less than $1200 a month in expenses until you reach 1 million liquid

62408643
>>62408593
If you have less than 1.5 million net worth you should not even think about buying a home. Plenty of studio apartments have washer and dryers now or live with your parents.

62408655
>>62408643
explain to me how paying rent ie giving SOMEONE ELSE equity is better than PAYING YOURSELF in equity
fucking rent seekers i swear to god

62408766
>>62408655
Starter homes start at 1m for humans.

62408780
>>62408766
delusional
i wish there were 60k or even 100k houses that weren't in the middle of a warzone, or looked like they were in one inside.

62408811
>>62408780
Not really. Starter homes for humans start at 1m. Every thing else is cope.

62409418
lol OP mass reported my thread what a massive fucking faggot ruined this board.

62409441
>>62407772
Have you heard of the Iraqi Dinar? That's what I would buy. If you can afford to gamble 4k on it revaluing, that's what I would do. The signs are all pointing to the revaluation happening soon
>t. shizo that trades based off dreams and visions

62409873
>>62407772
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62411502
>>62409418
after coming here and accusing me of being some guy from argentina, you make an off topic fake story thread that gets removed and then come back here from a new IP to blame me for "mass reporting" it and for "ruining" this board? Dude holy shit. I barely even look or post here anymore. No wonder it's gone to shit with mentally ill troons like you here. You need SERIOUS help, and should probably be isolated from society forever. Your mentally faculties are compromised beyond comprehension. You should leave this place forever, not just for our sake, but for your own sake. Even monkeys look down on you.

62411572
>>62411502
>off topic fake thread
None of what I said was fake, you instead mass reported me because you sit here 24/7 spamming larp threads and avoiding range bans and mass reporting absolutely everyone you literal fucking faggot.
This isn't your fucking board you kike nigger.

62411590
>>62410643
This is OP btw lol fucking begging faggot who mass reports anyone, then makes these humblebrag threads because he has 340k pesos.

62411597
>>62411502
Honestly I hope the next favela monkey you try fucking in the ass slits your throat, maybe then we can speak here Argentina anon, should I post your ID again so everyone can see who ruined this fucking board?

62411617
I've got OP's passport, his face, name, address, date of birth.
This caused him to flip the fuck out and ruined the board, I'm half tempted to use the pipeline to spam dox him nonstop until nobody can speak here ever again.

item.title: The entire western world order, for decades now, was custom built to produce this. — 13 replies — 52.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The entire western world order, for decades now, was custom built to produce this.

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-06-25 16:04 UTC

OP

The entire western world order, for decades now, was custom built to produce this. You, the young hard working man, must cut your teeth in school, rack up debt, in hard degrees, fight for internships, fight for junior level roles, prove yourself, for crumbs. For a little $80k job and a cuck shack and a promise that you might be able to retire in 40 years of soul crushing female daycare incorporated work.

But her? She can be an obese, retarded, shit for brained "former artist" with an associate's degree and the most laughable resume imaginable. Did her resume go in the trash? Of course not. She was fast tracked to the front. She got a cushy $300k+ executive job at a bank in NYC. And it's not just her. She's just the tip of the iceberg, the smoking gun. There are thousands, thousands, MILLIONS of women and POC like her who now enjoy sinecures at your expense. This is why the ladder was pulled up from under your hands. This is why you can't afford a family, can't be enfranchised, can't do your part and continue the American tradition.

Since the 1960's, everything that was done was to make sure this happened. You, the intelligent and enterprising young man, with designs for a family and full contribution to American glory, can never make it. But she, the obese, disgusting, litterbug sack of dogshit, can engorge yourself on the riches your ancestors built back during a time when they themselves were allowed to just work and contribute.

Remember, every time your resume is denied, when you're applying to 100+ jobs, when you're stuck in an entry level hole or even expected to do a full team's work while being paid modestly, there are endless amounts of Angie Baez's out there, above you, around you, robbing you of your birthright and your chance to continue a good thing. Everything must be degenerated instead, on behalf of the dregs of the world.

Early replies (first 3)

62410397
>>62410392
God has rewarded her ugly soul by making her ugly on the outside. Why should I care beyond that?

62410399
>$300k
That's a janitor salary there. It's probably 10x that. And 100x exit package. She's set for generations.

62410400
it’s disturbing that someone so fat would also go out of her way to look even more unattractive with the dumb braids, bizarre hat, shorts that show off the Penguin-like legs.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62410401
>>62410399
>>62410400
Oh nvm its another botted demotivational thread. Thanks op you suck monkey fuck

62410409
If you wouldn't bend her over a foam saddle and dick her down until your testicles broke, you are actually GAY.

62410412
>>62410392
Ok Lenin

62410415
>>62410401

Not a bot at all. This shit has me ready to march on my nearest kiked bank

62410420
I was already partially aware of the issue with banks in particular too. On my gf's side she has multiple female family members who are useless retards and had $200k+ managerial roles at a bank corpo, for some absurd reason. They literally don't know anything and one of them doesn't even have a degree.

It's way fucking worse than you could even ever believe. You can't have 4 kids and a house like your granddad because his role went to them.

62410515
>>62410392
>Baez also appears to have co-founded a queer and black, indigenous and people of color-owned talent agency, Same Page Co., which is “focused on increasing representation and equity in media and industry. It works with artists/talent on creative projects, photoshoots, strategy, and business affairs.”
Comedy. I'm sure she'll continue to fail upwards once enough people look away

62411511
Even if a trash can isn't bolted down or chained to a tree, a nigger or this fat disgusting piece of shit is going to take it

62411529
>>62410392
I also blame the jews

62411609
>>62410392
This is why I neet, I quit my job cause I was only making 80k and doing the job of 4 people. Meanwhile woman I worked with would do jack shit but still made 6 figures despite being useless. Neet is the only solution for this, if enough people neet every company will fail cause these useless woman can’t run anything.

62411635
All public property deserves to be stolen and destroyed. Privatize everything

item.title: BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!!! — 48 replies — 44.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!!!

Open thread • Replies: 48 • Updated: 2026-06-25 14:58 UTC

OP

>Today, Chainlink, FairSquareLab, a first-mover in core infrastructure within the Korean digital asset ecosystem, UniKA (Unified Korea Alliance), which represents the Korean coalition behind Project Pangea and is comprised of a steering committee of 5 entities (Shinhan Bank, JB Bank, Kbank, FairSquareLab, and OBDIA) along with 10+ participating Korean commercial banks, and Qivalis, a euro stablecoin consortium powered by 37 leading European banks, announced Project Pangea, a strategic, collaborative task force focused on redefining global foreign exchange markets by unlocking real-time, stablecoin-based cross-border settlement models.


https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/chainlink-and-multinational-banking-consortia-launch-project-pangea-to-develop-t0-settlement-framework-for-international-fx-markets-302807910.html

Early replies (first 3)

62401710
Wow I can’t wait for this to not mean anything.

62401722
p+c etc.
don't care until the needle moves on this FUCKING GODDAMN TOKEN

62401738
>>62401704
xrp sneeded?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62405567
>>62401710
Is this news big enough to dump Bitcoin or not?

62406112
>>62405567
It would seem so.

62407916
the chainlink llm must be well trained by now. the loop of
>generate buzzword headline
>gauge token buying response
>reinforce learning
has been going on for years, it's getting very effective

62408509
>>62403111
Trips of seething

62408891
>>62402814
>4 things
>4chan
illuminati confirmed

62409583
>>62406112
But Chino-chan, why would the price of Bitcoin have anything to do with the price of Chainlink?

62409593
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM SHAKALA. I am new to 4chan but have been loving this meme lately! I read on reddit yesterday that this would happen and I boughted (but unfortunately the price basically immediately soon after). I was still so pumped I went to Costco and got a slice of pizza and sat facing those walking out looking at everyone walking by knowing and rye-ly smiling knowing none of them understood what is happening. I felt like Brownstone Capital with Ben Rickerts in the Big Short. I had to stop myself from celebrating because I remembered they were missing out and I wanted to feel the same type of compassion they were reminded to feel by Ben in that moment so I finished my pizza solemnly leaving a bite out of clenched stomach respect for them not being able to finish my meal, grabbed my bag, put my hood up, and walked out nodding to the door inspector knowing looking at them knowingly and humanly as I walked out with them smiling not knowing beneath my shades how I was really seeing them. It was an amazing experience and it felt like everyone was watching but I know they weren't because the thing that made it special was that they don't understand or see. I will come back and tip the kitchen staff handsomely (especially the girl who I like but have not had the courage to ask out....yet) when we are making bookoo bucks and I can afford to own the whole store and promote her. and then smirk as I tell her I will sell the store, non-chalantly, so that I can ask her so she would feel comfortable if I ask her out on a date after I made her general manager of the store. I can't wait!

62409606
>>62409593
This might literally be the gayest, most link investor typified thing I have ever seen on this site in over a decade of lurking and posting all day and it is way too perfect to be a troll. Holy fucking shit.

62409613
>62409593
>62409606
Obvious samefag, absolutely pathetic lol

62409630
>>62409613
What does that mean (seems like a slur, no)? I just wanted to share a really awesome moment where I had a sort of micro-epiphany and specific sort of sonder imagining the future and knowing what we know, how most people just don't see. Why is this a bad thing? It was the best day I have had in a long time and I have been feeling really optimistic ever since and the awe I felt in this moment carried over really perfectly into the movie I went to see after (Disclosure Day), which had me thinking about how even though we see so much and are so knowledgeable, imagine what the aliens must see! There is probably some sort of alien Chainlink that other aliens zip around feeling this way about how we don't see or or maybe even a super Chainlink that they know that other aliens don't, if they aren't a hivemind super-intelligence or transcendent above the type of feeling or incapable of empathy at all. I just had a really deep moment and layed in bed thinking about it as well as how my revelation carried into the movie and then up up and away and I wanted to share it hoping it made someone else here think deep thoughts or feel optimistic too! We are going to be so freaking rich!!!

62409633
>what does that mean?
fucking newfags

62409639
>>62409633
Why do you guys keep calling me gay? I mean, I am bi but what's wrong with that? I am one of you! We should be able to band together Voltron this shit! LINK doesn't need us to for it to thrive but we might as well be in harmony as our investment carries us hand in hand to the moon!

62409640
>>62409606
>>62409613

62409661
>>62409640
How i feel in this thread! Meanwhile, I got the picture I am attaching to this comment in my back pocket, (which none of them know) just waiting to pull it out like the ultimate trump card. (¬‿ ͡¬) Dare I?

62409666
>>62406112
I don't know, but I sipped more link

62409669
>>62409666
>>62409583

62409675
>mB3FqHQS

62409679
>>62409675
Who is this? He looks crazy and scared. Is this from a movie or something? I don't recognize it. TL;DR?

62409681
>>62409675
I just saw you named the picture xrp. Are you saying this is XRP realizing the severity of what I posted in "62409661?" If so, post it again, because you are surely right!

62409739
i don't care anymore. they could do away with the token and send it to 0 and I would just post a LINK and picrel crossover thread to laugh at the copers who will be forever employed and get on with the rest of my life as if nothing happened and i didn't spend thkusands of hours reseaeching bullshit and making rarely reposted link memes for 7 years.
fuck you saturn

62409752
>>62409661
bless the undeserving, king

62409768
>>62409739
do things like that really happen though? No watchers, no justice? the whole token just poof?

62409835
>>62409752
*drops shades revealing the same knowing look from before, but sharper, and slams the picture down on table* BOOOOOM!

62409844
>>62409835

62409848
>>62409844
Exactly!

62409884
>>62409768
the token doesn't need monetary value to serve its technical purposes, you can incentivize everyone with other assets, staking has obviously been unnecessary to secure value etc. it's "happened" in all but name. but it might work out anyway, idk.

62409907
price

62410035
>>62409884
>the token doesn't need monetary value to serve its technical purposes
Same is true for Bitcoin, ETH, ...
Bitcoin was arguably way more secure when it was literal cents, and random faggots ran full mining nodes on their home PCs.

62410246
>mB3FqHQS
I've said it before and I'll say it again: the BOOOOOM meme absolutely destroyed some xerpies on here. Can't believe that after all the abuse and bullying they took for 10 years, simply posting the catchphrase of one of their twitter schizos is what did them in. Praying for you crazy bastards.

62411245
>>62410035
>duuuude it's not fair, bitcoin did xyz...

so how does it work in your head? 40 million altcoins should all have bitcoin or ethereum type price runup so things can become... fair?

you see folks, this is how the logic of a chainlink boyscout operates. no understanding that there isn't enough liquidity for their special altcoin to run like early crypto's did back when there was only like 50 of them. no understanding that obviously they aren't early anymore and therefore the days of those speculative runs are over.

instead of accepting the market they have, they cry like a child complaining to their parents that the other kids were allowed to have cookies before dinner.

they are: the few, the retarded... THE Cuckolds of crypto

item.title: Polkadot update — 33 replies — 30.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot update

Open thread • Replies: 33 • Updated: 2026-06-25 14:58 UTC

OP

ITS HERE

XRP, ETHEREUM AND LINK IS COOKED

https://medium.com/polkadot-network/dot-dao-and-the-need-for-jamkb-a069e72e9728

https://news.openshore.io/web3-summit-2026-recap.html

Early replies (first 3)

62399118
5. Privacy is a Competitive Advantage
In a world of surveillance capitalism, Polkadot's emphasis on privacy-by-default and user agency differentiates it sharply from other ecosystems. The proof-of-personhood protocol and private payment demonstrations showed concrete technical approaches, not just rhetoric.

Do they think big Google and Facebook will let them get away with this?

62399184
After further research

It seems we have a fork of consensus

https://forum.polkadot.network/t/introducing-a-new-jam-token/13029

https://medium.com/polkadot-network/dot-dao-and-the-need-for-jamkb-a069e72e9728

So what the fuck is JAM and how will it bring value back to DOT marketcap?

62399190
>The JAM protocol presupposes the existence of a token to ensure soundness in its state usage. I argue that DOT would make a highly suboptimal choice and that a new, fixed-issuance token to represent the new Polkadot resource of JAM state makes the most sense. Assuming DOT DAO were to launch a Polkadot JAM for its upgrade, then this new resource-access token would be wholly owned by DOT DAO by default. DOT utility would continue as at present, implying control of DOT DAO — and with it, effective ownership of the Polkadot JAM — as well as the means for staking, collateral and coretime payment within the Polkadot ecosystem.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62399197
>>62399190
oh boy, another scam

62399251
Slurped

62399395
>>62399197

Linkie crying seeing another crypto vibing

62400350
>>62399184
a new resource is being introduced to Polkadot with the JAM upgrade (fast shared storage like RAM) and you need one JAMKB per KB of RAM you want to use on the network, so you can't buy more tokens than there are KBs of RAM

62400419
>>62399084
DOT will make us all rich beyond our Wildest dreams!!

62401678
>>62399084
I'll believe they're serious when I see this on an fdroid repository

62401689
>>62401678
they had a grapheneOS build for the event so it is likely that will happen

62401700
>>62399197
Cope

62401739
>>62401689
Interesting
Just bought a few k
We'll see how this goes

62401877
>>62401739
A lambo will be in your future

62401946
>>62401877
Checked.

62402307
>>62399251
You have a missed call

62402326
>>62402317
Didn't NEO have a similar issue

62402419
>>62402317
it's just an nft with price discovery so they don't have to do all of that coretime auction bullshit again

62403350
>>62402317

Thats not even what its use is for

62403448
>>62402317

im surprised dot isnt trading at 10 dollars considering the pedigree

but they overspent on marketing during a bear market instead of 2021. but on the plus side the dot treasury has started reporting quarterly profits. Unlike Cardano or Chainlink

it wont stay under a dollar for long

62404229
>>62403448
>it wont stay under a dollar for long

Under 50 cents by 2027

62404438
0.87 new low lmao. god what a piece of shit.

62404588

I dont care

62404599
>>62404229

62405376
>>62401877

I've heard that before
We'll see
Been waiting for an actual parallel economy system for fucking ever
Was watching komodo and loki/oxen before this and they never got off the ground

62405404

62405433
Didn't read but is he hugging DOT and shilling a new token?

62406887

62408015

62408521

Why not DCA?

item.title: I lost alL my family savings. I NEED HELP — 9 replies — 18.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
I lost alL my family savings. I NEED HELP

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-06-25 15:35 UTC

OP

Please someone help. I put the money in Binary Options and FOR SOME FCKIN REASON I end up LOSING IT ALL LIKE WTF? IT WAS SUPPOSED TO INCREASE, I DID MY STUDY, MY INDICATORS, ALL OF IT AND THE FUCKING CHART KEPT GOING AGAINST ME. I LOST EVERYTHING, MY WIFE DOESNT KNOW YET, HOW CAN I SOLVE THIS????????

Early replies (first 3)

62410686
>>62410643
1. Stop being a bitch
2. 100x btc now faggot.
3. Make it.

62410984
>>62410643
>someone help
How?
Are you begging for donations?

62411016
>>62410643
admit what happened to your wife
beg for forgiveness
get a job

this is the hardest part of starting over.

62411036
>>62410643
no crying in the casino

62411261
>>62410643
Admit to yourself and your wife that you're a gambler. That's the only way to have a chance of keeping her. But I doubt you are able to act accordingly, because you are a gambler in denial.

62411269
>>62410643
Just be yourself and it'll work itself out

62411322
>>62410984
Of course he is he's a catspamming faggot who mass reported me for asking for ways to come up with 3k in a month while having a failing heart, then he reports my thread and makes 10 begging threads on his own thinking somehow this will work.
Nobody will even help a dying man what makes you OP think anyone will help your annoying faggot ass.

62411420
>>62410643
How much? Post your trade so at least I can finish

62411485
>>62410643
You should have just invested in BTC, getting too greedy makes you lose everything, I lost all my savings on memes too but now I'm going all into BTC and will lend it out if I need liquidity when TBV is live

item.title: CSAM kills Bitcoin — 13 replies — 7.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
CSAM kills Bitcoin

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-06-25 14:20 UTC

OP

The first transaction that includes CSAM and is replicated across nodes will permanently tank Bitcoin's price.

The clock is ticking.

Early replies (first 3)

62410005
>>62410002
Adam looks glow, Gaylor is glowing too, is Lee a glow operation too?
Btvlw wtf is this in non alien vitalik language?

62410008
the missing use case already kills BTC lol. that is just desecrating the corpse

62410011
>>62410002
Not to mention once its committed it will be there forever, making the blockchain illegal to possess.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62410036
>>62410002
it's fucking over

62410149
>>62410002
>The first transaction that includes CSAM
You assume this hasn't happened before?

62410186
>>62410002
The guy who was paid by Epstein and went to his island to probably rape children warns that OP_RETURN, a central key of the global chain, may attract child porn. lol

BSV is Bitcoin

62410228
>>62410149
>80 bytes
attached is a 1x1 pixel white square at maximum compression. it is 1228 bytes. so no.

62410231
>>62410002
you are weird

62410239
>>62410228
I could jerk off to that. I grew up in the 90s.

62410241
>>62410228
It's 628B you nigger

62410243
>>62410228
Sure, for a 32 bit color image with headers.

80 bytes could hold a raw 8 bit black and white image with 80 pixels. With compression possibly slightly larger. One bit mono image could be be greater than 640 pixels with compression. Over multiple transactions you could definitely upload a picture that can be pieced back together pretty easily.

62410689
>>62410002
Why didn't it tank fiat then?

62410991
>>62410002
Imagine thinking that something that relies on "developers" is going to have long term value.

item.title: /smg/ - stock market genera — 449 replies — 607.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
/smg/ - stock market genera

Open thread • Replies: 449 • Updated: 2026-06-25 15:20 UTC

OP

Almost time edition

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html
https://www.livestreamy.net/bloomberg/

>Charts:
https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

>Screeners:
https://finviz.com/
https://etfdb.com/

>Gambling:
https://www.optionsplaybook.com/options-introduction/
https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com
https://optionstrat.com/
https://www.optionistics.com/quotes/option-prices

>Pre-Market and Live data:
https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures
https://finance.yahoo.com/

>Calendars:
https://www.marketwatch.com/economy-politics/calendar
https://www.earningswhispers.com/calendar
https://www.cmegroup.com/trading/interest-rates/countdown-to-fomc.html
https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

>Boomer Investing 101:
https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started
https://www.sec.gov/search-filings

>Misc:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates

>Previous: >>62409057

Early replies (first 3)

62410121

62410125
>>62410116

62410127

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62410978
Very organic dump

62410979
>>62410966
yeah its over
we will go under the june 9th low wirh this strong selloff
should solded my puts

62410980
microslop seems like a good buy now, theres no further downside
right..?

62410983
>hmm I wonder how the Roth is doing today
>up 0.2%
Zzz, not nearly enough volatility

62410985
Cash kings how we doin'

62410988
>>62410794
how? i tried rocm shit few months aho and didnt work with linux subsystem under win

62410990
Send out another wave of volatility Kevin.

62410992
>>62410977
Literally all you have to do is buy META/GOOG/AMZN/MSFT today and turn your app off for a year and you will be up 60%

62410993
did shiki shitter got banned?

62410998
this is it, I am out. weekend for me and the weather is nice. I dont need this faggy wallstreet shit fucking up my mood.

62411000
>all this volatility
>VTV stayed up 2.00%
value chads, sound off

62411001
>>62410985
good
almost went all in on mu before opening

62411002
Oracle keeps dumping

62411003
hold you paper handed niggers
its a final shakeout/ buy up for the institutions

62411004
>>62410980
Non meme answer is that it just annihilated expectations in a good way, when it already had near universal consensus that it would continue to rise. Other memory stocks (WDC, STX, SNDK) will also benefit since the driving force seems to just be the supply squeeze getting worse and margins getting higher. I actually have no idea why WDC and STX aren't up 10% or more right now, probably a better buy than MU at this moment

62411008
((((they)))) won't allow us to have a mega pump

62411009
Lots of volatility, but it's crabatility, not the good kind

62411011
>>62411000
I'm surprised I don't see more investors in AVGV. I don't own it but I've considered it.

62411014
>>62410980
MFST is down because they were the biggest loser of the AI race. (Huge CapEx, with very little AI successes to show for it)
Nothing about today changes the CapEx part of that.

62411015
>>62411012
>>62411012
>>62411012

62411019
It’s a delicious irony that semiconductors ended up financially hurting everyone ruining the world. Dot com wasn’t AI failing it was AI succeeding too much making everything impossible expensive.

62411022
>>62411004
Think he was talking about Microsaarft

62411029
>>62411003
actually this is the start of #TheBigOne

62411032
>>62411014
Oh he meant MSFT

They're doing a lot better than Google at the very least. Anthropic and OpenAI IPOs will be the way to go on direct LLM investment though.

62411047
>>62411003
Running up futures and then rugging is usually retail shitting their pants and top calling for the ten trillionth time in the open, rather than some conspiracy.

62411058
>>62411047
Um no clearly this is the start of another dotcom bubble burst, silly mumu

62411079
>>62411058
True Dotcom bubble where we had rapid rises of companies with 0 earnings is exactly the same as memory stocks earnings climbing thousand of % due to a completely changing paradigm and selling a constrained key component of a supply chain.

62411130
>>62411079
I think there's risks to capex spend but I think you're delusional if you think mu plodding along at 8x forward earnings looks like csco in dotcom doing 150x.
The AI names themselves also have a better shot at profitability than dotcom where people were quite literally making up new metrics to justify valuations with no money and no plan to make money.

62411229
>>62410993
Worse, liquidated

62411387
>>62410583
test

item.title: VOO and Chill — 75 replies — 61.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 75 • Updated: 2026-06-25 14:51 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62405252
>>62405201
Are you replying to the wrong post all that guy did was ask questions

62405400
>>62404617
they predicted a 5-6% YTD gain for 2026 and we're already above that, lying shits.

62406764
>>62400493
Rather buy VT. Not so bullish on USA anymore

62406776
>>62406764
Still, conceptually it’s sound right?

62406804
>>62400493
>QQQ
if you want more returns (with more risk), you should do something like leverage + vt (or similar) instead of voo (you should at least do vti) and global small cap value, not sector etfs

62406811
>>62406804
in English doc

62406843
>>62406811
Alright! I'm gonna explain it like you're 5 years old!

Think of investing like choosing a basket of seeds: VOO is like only planting the biggest U.S. trees, VTI is like planting the whole U.S. garden, VT is like planting the whole world garden, and global small cap value is like adding lots of tiny, scrappy plants that have sometimes grown faster over long periods; if you want more possible growth and can stomach bigger ups and downs, using leverage is like turning up the volume on a diversified basket, which can help more than just buying one loud sector ETF, because sectors are a bet on one part of the economy and can stay out of favor for a long time, while broad diversification plus leverage is usually a cleaner way to aim for higher returns without relying on one slice of the market.

62406996
>>62406804
I am a noob and leverage seems scary desu like I will owe that money eventually right

62407040
>>62406996
yes, I'm not into leverage either, it evolves its own risks, but if you are not risk averse, it's better than gambling/prediction markts/crypto etc.

62407059
>>62400493
Voo used to pay more dividends before it was all fag7. Its basically the same as the nasdaq for the past decade

62407078
>>62400493
yeah I have like almost half a million in VTI right now, and I just need to sit on it for another 10 years. Its funny, I get excited whenever VTI goes down because it means i get a little bonus next deposit

62407086
Man I want to just have more principal to invest rather than chasing returns
Anyone know of good side hustles or AI grifts anyone can do
Idc as long as it’s triple digits a month at least

62407097
>>62406776
with Trump's economy + trade wars, VXUS actually went up a ton during the period and now a lot of countries are getting less and less dependent on America. If/when the AI bubble ever pops then you're going to be a lot more insulated since the American economy is being propped up by it.

62409216
>>62407097
Makes sense but aren’t all global economies just going to be downstream of American AI / Computing companies turning a profit?

62409221
>>62402005
pr34ch

62409463
>>62400515
>t. down 95% in fartniggercoin

62409479
>ETF and chill is smart but I'm smarter I can time the market!

62409545
>>62400493
honestly everything is fucked. index fund bagholders are forced to buy shitstocks and act like paypiggies for pump and dumpers like elon musk. it's literally causing a bubble. nobody wins except billionaires who force your grandma's retirement fund to pay for their malfunctioning AI.

62409811
>low fee index funds
it's VTI and VXUS for me

62409901
>>62400636
lol doomer, stop spreading negativity. you will be a lot happier even if you just invested 150 a month.

62410053
>>62409901
>Realism is negativity!

62410067
>>62400493
wtf are you doing bringing common sense into my retard and bot zoo?

62410086
>>62400636
>The problem is we have people starting at age 35 with like 150 a month thinking it's going to somehow mean something.
pop quiz! how much would you expect your money to grow over 150 months (12.5 years) with a conservative 6% annual return?

62410375
Yep just bought another share of VOO

62410405
>>62403053
They are also expecting SS and medicare to buff out their income and reduce their costs. Everyone here is likely planning to have no SS or other government pension.

62410414
>>62402995
>>62403044
Really you two are dumb as fuck. Do you not understand what FIRE is?

VOO has 15% CAGR since inception. 11.75% after inflation. With even a generous withdrawal rate you can live very comfortably on it if you have enough capital. My business partner does this, goes on vacations all the time. He has a couple of million invested across UK-equivalent ETFs like VUSA. Loads of people do this, it's not the absolute best use of capital but it's a good return for how liquid and low fee it is.
>>62403044
Learn to read you fucking imbecile. I posted that only millionaires CAN LIVE OFF IT. . Anyone can invest and make gains from ETFs but you won't get rich unless you invest for decades.
>Also getting rich quick is literally the point of all this
The point of what? Investing? No it isn't. The point of ETFs? LOL. You are just some dumb teen shitcoiner with sky high time preference I can just tell. Go and buy more shitcoins so you can "get rich quick" dickhead.

62410968
>>62410414
This is why I just wanna get as much capital in there as soon as possible it just seems like something I can actually control versus chasing returns
Don’t have any ideas though

62410981
>>62407086
how about a job nigger

62411103
>1obitd
Omfg jannies do your fucking job niggers.

62411192
>>62410981
I am currently at my full time salary job right now silly

item.title: Why aren't you holding BTC? — 58 replies — 55.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why aren't you holding BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 58 • Updated: 2026-06-25 15:01 UTC

OP

Too expensive? Prefer alts? Don't trust it? Or do you simply think there are better opportunities elsewhere?
Curious to hear your thoughts.

Early replies (first 3)

62395695
you can get better gains on stocks. those companies work for your money. btc sits there doing nothing but has the same risk profile as sharing a hobos needle without greater fools left to carry it to $200k

62395915
give me reasons to hold btc.

62395925
I am holding because I bought in 2020 before etfs now I refuse to sell because I'll get audit raped by the CRA supposedly so I'm stuck holding until it's got better tax laws or borrowing rates.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62403095
>>62396002
>Invest only in actual products made by actual companies.
What are you investing?

62404024
>>62398163
Bank transfer? Hmmm

62404122
If you're not holding BTC when it is clear that the fiat system is on its last days you are beyond retarded.

62404297
>>62395655

Big Thai Cock?
Well I mean like why aren't YOU holding it?
I mean.

62404304
>why aren't you holding goy gold
/biz/ is already out of crypto.
https://web.archive.org/web/202602162103...f-bitcoin/

62404487
>>62404304
Lol

62404614
>>62404122
Why do you need fiat?

62405784
>>62401190
Why though? If you can earn from your BTC while maintaining self-custody, I don't see the problem.

62405818
>>62398819
If it's still BTC and I still control the keys, I don't see why it stops being Bitcoin.

62406014
>>62395655
>Why aren't you holding BTC?
I will buy when it's cheaper. 100 bucks seems like a fair value for the future of finance.

62406579
>>62399676
I guess not but..

62406783
There is simply no reason for it to have any value boss.

62409051
>>62399898
You are a fucking retard. The current price means NOTHING. Its NOTHING! Lower the better to buy more.
Boomers are still in the seats of power. They dont embrace BTC because most boomers not in some kind of power see it as not something they can walk into a bank and ask their financial advisor to get. Therefore its fake and gay to them.
The boomers being the biggest voting block, are the most catered to. So basically the reason why the world has not been embracing digital is because of these fucking idiots.
Once boomers are out of the seats of power and fucking gone and millennials are in charge its going to get real digital.BTC will inevitably be embraced.
More countries will just adopt it as their main currency like El Salvador. Its the best logical option. Your economy is crumbling and the nations dollar is shit? Just adopt bitcoin its quite simple.

Not only that, but owning a Bitcoin will be so rare, everything will cost in Satoshis. Once Amazon adopts a bitcoin logo payment option its 10000% on. Once Musk post some stupid twitter picture of a moon bitcoin mining farm its going to moon.

Get it now while its cheap. Price has no meaning at the moment. If your a day trader I get why you may be pessimistic about it. But if your a hodler and believe in BTC Philosophically and not thinking of it like a share/stock then thats the right idea.

BTC is known by everyone. If you went to what ever hood you are in and asked the most wet cigarette smelling schizo hobo " Hey have you heard of that digital gold thing" the response would be some schizo babble with "That bitcoin stuff:"

Its backed by million computers. It takes IRL power/electrical energy and time (mining) to produce. Thats what money realy is or better to say what gives something value. Time+Energy. Its basically electricity converted into money. And electricity is not free.

HODL is the right idea for a boomerless digital embraced future. dont let Day Traders do it for quick buck not future.

62409106
>>62395695
>"those companies work for your money."
>babby's first dable on stocks
>doesn't know owners are diluting your investment and buying mansions with the ATM

62409107
>>62399898
To add to this, You are thinking of like 10x+ full BTC holders. Average persons goal is to have a single one or 2 to just hold on to the future.
The scenario you are talking about is like for some kind of rich guy living on the lamb which is like 1 out of 10 million people.

The real persons goal is to get a bitcoin and maybe it will be like 300,000 per coin in the future. Then you have enough for your familys college or whatever. Just a good foundation for a son/daughters future. Not somthing you just cash out to cartoonishly and buy lambos with and post videos of your garage with hexagon light ceilings.

62409236
>>62405784
You know it's a risky move.

62409560
>>62409236
Depends what you mean by risky. I wouldn't touch an unaudited protocol, but I also don't think every new protocol carries the same level of risk.

62409564
>>62399898
Governments taxing something doesn't make it dead. If anything, it usually means they've accepted it's an asset class that's here to stay.

62409803
>>62409560
More like defi hacks

62409813
>>62409107
It's possible 10 years from now. Pass it to your kids

62409997
>>62409803
That's why I'm watching Babylon instead of blindly aping into random protocols. They've been taking audits and testing seriously.

62410003
>>62406014
People have gone broke waiting for the perfect entry just as often as they've bought too early.

62410039
>>62410003
So what's the best time to buy

62410525
>>62410039
everytime, imo.

62411160
>>62410525
I don't have money

62411213
>>62409997
I think their TBVs could unlock a lot of Bitcoin use cases that weren't really possible before. We'll know a lot more once the Aave V4 public testnet is complete tho.

62411224
>>62409051
Pls summary

62411241
>>62396002
What About gold and silver?

62411256
>>62404304
I'm not convinced .

62411265
I will just keep my 0.3 btc somewhere
